businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and commercial AI data analytics platform leader with Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise. FY2025 revenue reached $4.48B (+60%). US commercial revenue exceeded $650M (Q4 +137% YoY). Pentagon adopted Maven AI as an official program of record for military-wide deployment. Selected as software developer for the $185B Golden Dome missile defense project. Palantir tools are in active combat use during the Iran war.
howTheyMakeMoney: Enterprise AI platform (Foundry) - large enterprise data integration
AIP (AI Platform) - generative AI integration platform
AIP Bootcamps - rapid onboarding programs for customer conversion

keyMetrics.name: Trailing P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: 6.3x the S&P 500 IT sector avg of 34x. 4.8x the software sector avg of 45x. Still extreme premium despite 3-day 12% drop

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E (FY2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS est of $1.22. Per Stocktwits, 5-month low. Still 3x above growth stock avg of 30-40x

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (FY2026E)
keyMetrics.interpretation: From $4.48B (FY2025) to projected $7.40B. War ending could slow defense revenue acceleration

keyMetrics.name: P/S Ratio
keyMetrics.interpretation: 5-7x premium vs software sector avg of 10-15x. Slightly improved on decline

keyMetrics.name: YTD Return
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down 22% YTD. 33.7% off 52-week high of $207.52. Three-day 12% drop erased most defense rally gains

keyMetrics.name: RSI
keyMetrics.interpretation: Approaching oversold territory (30). 247WallSt: 'stock looks oversold.' Three-day 12% drop pushed RSI down sharply

growthDrivers.title: 247WallSt: 'Oversold, is 89% gain realistic?' Citi $260, Wedbush $230 (3/31, today)
growthDrivers.description: 247WallSt published oversold analysis today. Citi's Tyler Radke at $260 (+89%), Wedbush's Dan Ives at $230 (+68%). 'AIP is becoming the enterprise AI standard regardless of war developments'

growthDrivers.title: Trump signals Iran war ending (3/31, double-edged sword)
growthDrivers.description: WSJ: Trump indicates willingness to wind down hostilities. S&P +1.1%. Double-edged for PLTR: macro stabilization helps tech stocks, but war ending could accelerate defense premium erosion

growthDrivers.title: Stellantis 5-year partnership renewal and expansion (3/30)
growthDrivers.description: Palantir renewed and expanded its partnership with Stellantis, the world's 4th largest auto group. Demonstrates Foundry platform's enterprise customer expansion

growthDrivers.title: Golden Dome $185B missile defense software (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Palantir and Anduril selected for Trump administration's $185B Golden Dome missile defense system software. This is long-term infrastructure that persists regardless of war status

growthDrivers.title: Maven AI military-wide program of record (3/23)
growthDrivers.description: Pentagon designated Maven Smart System as official program of record. Long-term military deployment confirmed independent of any specific conflict

growthDrivers.title: Polymarket AI anti-fraud partnership (3/26)
growthDrivers.description: Palantir's Vergence AI handles Polymarket's real-time trading surveillance and user behavior monitoring. Entry point into financial services

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ years of government defense AI track record, highest-level security clearances, Maven military-wide adoption, combat-proven in Iran war. A moat money can't buy

moats.description: Ontology platform deeply integrated into government/military systems. Replacement costs are prohibitively high
Highest-level clearances and 20+ years of government trust. Virtually impossible for new entrants to replicate
Active use in Iran war for real-time targeting. Maven adoption ensures demand beyond any single conflict
AIP Bootcamps drive rapid customer acquisition. Ontology data model ecosystem expands with each deployment

competitors.name: Anduril Industries
competitors.detail: Defense AI/drone startup. Partner on Golden Dome. $60B valuation

competitors.name: Snowflake / Databricks
competitors.detail: Enterprise data platform competitors. Far behind on government/defense access

competitors.name: Microsoft / AWS
competitors.detail: Cloud giants pursuing government AI. Cannot match Ontology's depth of integration

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI platform. Significantly trails Palantir in revenue scale and growth

recentNews.headline: 247WallSt: 'Palantir looks oversold, is 89% year-ahead gain realistic?'
recentNews.significance: Down a third from highs. Citi $260 (+89%), Wedbush $230 (+68%) maintained. 'AIP is becoming enterprise AI standard.' Sell-side standing firm despite selloff

recentNews.headline: Trump signals Iran war ending, S&P surges +1.1%
recentNews.significance: Double-edged for PLTR. Macro stabilization helps tech stocks broadly, but war ending could accelerate defense premium erosion. Gulf states want war to continue (AP), adding uncertainty

recentNews.headline: Stocktwits: 'PLTR drops 12% in 3 days, is the defense boost over?'
recentNews.significance: Three-day 12% decline erased most post-war defense rally. Forward P/E at 5-month low. Sentiment turned bearish

recentNews.headline: Parameter.io: 'PLTR -3.85% to $137.55 despite Stellantis 5-year deal'
recentNews.significance: Fell on day of positive news. Macro selling pressure overwhelmed fundamentals. War-ending news could enable today's reversal

recentNews.headline: TradingKey: 'PLTR -3.81%, Peter Thiel and executives selling through March'
recentNews.significance: Thiel and executives selling continuously in March. 10b5-1 plan counterargument, but psychologically damaging

analystOpinions.notableComment: Citi $260 (+89%), Wedbush $230 (+68%), Mizuho $195 Outperform. 247WallSt: 'oversold territory.' MarketBeat consensus $198.59 (+44.4%)

risks.title: War ending accelerates defense premium erosion
risks.description: Trump's war-ending signal could strip remaining defense premium faster. Three-day 12% decline already gave back most war rally. Confirmed war ending could add further downside pressure

risks.title: Extreme valuation at P/E 215x
risks.description: P/E 215x (6.3x IT sector average). With defense premium fading, justification becomes even harder. Motley Fool: needs 330%+ earnings growth to bring P/E to 50x

risks.title: Peter Thiel + executive continuous insider selling in March
risks.description: Per TradingKey, Thiel and executives sold throughout March. May have anticipated defense premium erosion from potential war ending

risks.title: Analyst opinion split
risks.description: 17 Buy, 14 Hold, 5 Sell. Market skepticism on valuation persists. War-ending news could strengthen bearish consensus

risks.title: Gulf states want war continuation, ending uncertain
risks.description: AP: Gulf states demand war until Iran is no longer a threat. Actual ending is more complex than Trump's signal

buyReasons.title: 247WallSt 'oversold' + Citi $260 (+89%)
buyReasons.rationale: Down 33.7% from highs, RSI 38, three-day 12% drop reaches oversold territory. Citi and Wedbush maintain high targets based on AIP's long-term enterprise value

buyReasons.title: Macro stabilization is positive for tech stocks broadly
buyReasons.rationale: War ending improves overall macro environment, potentially ending the tech stock selloff. PLTR's Foundry/commercial segment clearly benefits

buyReasons.title: Maven and Golden Dome are war-independent long-term contracts
buyReasons.rationale: Maven is a program of record, permanently adopted across the military. Golden Dome is $185B missile defense infrastructure. Contracts persist regardless of war status

buyReasons.title: Stellantis 5-year renewal, commercial revenue accelerating
buyReasons.rationale: Commercial revenue Q4 +137% YoY. Stellantis 5-year deal proves enterprise expansion. Reduces war-dependency narrative

overallOpinion: Trump's war-ending signal (WSJ) is a double-edged sword for Palantir. S&P surged 1.1%, potentially ending the tech selloff, but defense premium erosion could accelerate. The stock already gave back most war rally gains with a 12% drop in three days

247WallSt published an oversold analysis today, noting Citi's $260 (+89%) and Wedbush's $230 (+68%) targets remain intact. Maven and Golden Dome are war-independent long-term contracts. But P/E 215x with Thiel's continuous March selling remains a significant overhang

At $137.55, below 50-day ($149) and 200-day ($169). RSI 38. War ending could trigger short-term additional decline but macro stabilization could form a medium-term floor. Hold, accumulate small positions after macro stabilizes

investmentVerdict.summary: Trump's war-ending signal is a double-edged sword: defense premium erosion (negative) vs macro stabilization (positive). Maven and Golden Dome are war-independent contracts, and Citi $260 target reflects AIP's long-term enterprise value. However, P/E 215x, Thiel selling, and 12% three-day decline are headwinds. Accumulate small positions after macro confirmation

sources.description: Thiel 2M share sale is 2% of total, pre-scheduled plan

witty.hook: War is ending and PLTR's defense magic might end with it. P/E 215x sweats
witty.text:
$PLTR $137.55(-3.85%)

Trump: 'Ending the war.'
Defense stocks: '...'
3 days, -12%. The market got the memo early.
Defense premium? Going, going...

1/4
thread:
Citi still says $260 (+89%).
'AIP is the standard, war or not.'
Maven stays. Golden Dome stays.
Only the hype leaves.

2/4

Thiel sold all March. Coincidence?
P/E 215x looking for a reason to exist.
Commercial Q4 +137% is the reason.

3/4

Target $199(+44%). Hold.
Buy when the premium fully washes out.

4/4
